title: acmella fimbriata
intro: Acmella fimbriata, also known as toothache plant or paracress, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, particularly its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as spilanthol and acmellin. The health benefits of Acmella fimbriata include pain relief, reduced inflammation, and antioxidant properties, making it a potential treatment for various conditions such as toothaches, arthritis, and skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Acmella fimbriata are typically used fresh or dried and can be made into teas, infusions, or added to topical creams and ointments.
